overview: The JSON Filter extracts translatable text from JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) files based on the [JSON specification](http://www.json.org/). It supports extracting all key-value pairs or selective extraction via regex path rules, and can map JSON keys to translation unit identifiers (`resname` in XLIFF). The filter also supports comments (though not technically legal in JSON): `//`, `#`, `/* */`, and `<!-- -->` styles.

extraction.extractAll.help:
Controls whether all key-value string pairs are extracted for translation.

- **When true** (default): all string values with associated keys are extracted. Use `excludeKeys` to skip specific keys.
- **When false**: no key-value pairs are extracted unless they match the `pathRules` regex.

The `excludeKeys` field acts as an exception list — its behavior **inverts** based on this setting:
- If extracting all: keys matching the exception are **excluded**
- If not extracting all: keys matching the exception are **included**

extraction.pathRules.help:
Regex pattern matched against the **full key path** (e.g. `/widgets/body.*`). When specified, this **overrides** the `extractAll` toggle and the `excludeKeys` exceptions — only keys matching this regex are extracted.

Paths use `/` separators matching the JSON nesting structure.
extraction.pathRules.values: Java regex matched against full JSON key paths like `/menu/value/popup/menuitem/value`.
examples: `/widgets/body.*` — extract all keys under `widgets` starting with `body`

dependsOn.condition: overrides extractAll and excludeKeys when specified

extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.help: Extracts string values that appear in arrays without an associated key. By default, only key-value pairs are considered for extraction — standalone strings in arrays are skipped.

extraction.excludeKeys.help:
Keys listed here behave as **exceptions** to the `extractAll` setting:

- If `extractAll` is **true**: matching keys are **excluded** from extraction
- If `extractAll` is **false**: matching keys are **included** for extraction

When `useFullPath` is enabled, you can match against the full nested path.
extraction.excludeKeys.values: Comma-separated key names or, when combined with `useFullPath`, regex patterns like `^.*?/excludedStructure/.*`
examples: `^.*?/excludedStructure/.*` — with `useFullPath`, exclude all nested elements under `excludedStructure`

extraction.subfilterRules.help:
Specify an Okapi filter ID (e.g. `okf_html`) to process the content of all translatable text values with that sub-filter. Leave blank for default behavior.

Useful when JSON values contain embedded markup (e.g. HTML snippets).
extraction.subfilterRules.values: An Okapi filter ID such as `okf_html`, `okf_xmlstream`, etc.
notes: This option **cannot** be used together with the inline codes option — they are mutually exclusive.

keys.useAsName.help: Uses the JSON key as the `resname` (resource name) of the extracted text unit. This provides a stable identifier for matching translations back to their source entries.

keys.useFullPath.help:
Uses the full nested key path (e.g. `/menu/value/popup/menuitem/value`) as the text unit name instead of just the immediate key.

When enabled, the `excludeKeys` regex patterns also apply against the **full path**, allowing more precise filtering of nested structures.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to take effect

keys.leadingSlash.help:
Controls whether the full key path starts with a `/` character. Only relevant when `useFullPath` is enabled.

Example: `/menu/value` vs `menu/value`

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when useFullPath is true

keys.useIdStack.help: Builds text unit IDs by stacking nested keys. This provides hierarchical IDs that reflect the JSON structure.

keys.idRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **values** become the text unit ID (`resname` in XLIFF), overriding the `useAsName` behavior.

Useful when your JSON contains explicit ID fields alongside translatable text.
examples: Given `{"key": "datePicker_marchMonth", "text": "March"}`, the regex `key` causes `datePicker_marchMonth` to become the `resname` of the `text` value's text unit.

metadata.noteRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **values** are attached as translator notes (`<note>` elements in XLIFF) to the associated text unit.

Useful for passing context, descriptions, or comments to translators.
metadata.noteRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
examples: `/widgets/name.*` — values of matching keys become notes on the text unit

metadata.metaRules.help: Regex matching keys whose **values** are added as generic metadata (`<context-group>` elements in XLIFF) to the associated text unit.
metadata.metaRules.values: Java regex matched against key names or full paths.
examples: `/widgets/image.*` — matched key:value pairs are added as metadata

metadata.maxwidthRules.help:
Regex matching keys whose **numeric values** set the `maxwidth` property on the XLIFF `<trans-unit>`, enabling length restrictions for translations.

- The matched value applies as `maxwidth` to **all other elements at the same array level**
- Only **one** matching element per hierarchy level is allowed
- For nested arrays, different parent-child and sibling levels can define different maxwidth values
- If no key matches at a sub-level but one exists at a higher level, the higher-level value applies to the **deepest** hierarchy level only
- When keys match at both higher and lower levels, the higher-level matching key must appear **after** the last child element
metadata.maxwidthRules.values: Java regex matched against key names.
notes: Only one matching array element for the regex is allowed on each hierarchy level.
When keys match at both higher and lower levels, the higher-level matching key must be defined **after** the last child element.

maxwidthSizeUnit.help: The value entered here is used as the `size-unit` attribute on XLIFF `<trans-unit>` elements that have length restrictions (from `maxwidthRules`).
maxwidthSizeUnit.values: A string value for the XLIFF `size-unit` attribute (e.g. `char`, `pixel`, `byte`).

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when maxwidthRules is configured

output.escapeSlashes.help: Controls whether forward slashes (`/`) are escaped as `\/` in the output JSON. Some JSON producers escape slashes (especially in URLs or HTML within JSON), while others do not.

inlineCodes.help:
Enables detection of inline codes within extracted text using regex patterns. Any match is converted to an inline code placeholder in the extracted content.

The default pattern detects:
- `printf`-style format specifiers (e.g. `%s`, `%d`, `%2$s`)
- Escape sequences (`\n`, `\t`, `\r\n`, etc.)
- Curly-brace placeholders (e.g. `{0}`, `{name}`)
notes: This option **cannot** be used together with the sub-filter option — they are mutually exclusive.

processingNotes: The filter supports any encoding, not just Unicode. It detects encoding via: (1) Unicode BOM, (2) `charset` declaration in the first 1000 characters, (3) fallback to the default encoding.
If output encoding is UTF-8 and input was also UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input. If input was a different encoding, no BOM is written.
Output line-breaks match the original input line-break style.
Though not technically legal in JSON, the filter supports `//`, `#`, `/* */`, and `<!-- -->` comment styles.
limitations: Comments within a JSON **string value** are parsed as part of the string content, not as comments. A configured sub-filter will then process these as true comments (they become part of the skeleton).

examples.title: Basic JSON extraction
examples.description: A simple JSON file with nested translatable values. With default settings (`extractAll: true`), all string values associated with keys are extracted.

examples.title: Using ID rules with key-value arrays
examples.description: When your JSON uses a separate `key` field to identify entries (common in flat translation formats), configure `idRules` with the regex `key` to use those values as the text unit `resname`.

examples.title: Selective extraction with path rules
examples.description: Use `pathRules` to extract only specific sections of a large JSON file. This overrides the `extractAll` and `excludeKeys` settings.
examples.config:
```yaml
extraction:
  pathRules: "/widgets/body.*"
metadata:
  noteRules: "/widgets/name.*"
  metaRules: "/widgets/image.*"
keys:
  idRules: "/widgets/id.*"
```
